## Freelance Services Agreement (Brand Refresh 2025)

### Preamble

This Freelance Services Agreement ("Agreement") is made and entered into as of January 15, 2025 ("Effective Date") by and between TechStartup Inc., a company for which Sarah Johnson serves as Marketing Director ("Client"), and Chen Creative Studio, for which Alex Chen is a Senior Designer ("Freelancer"). The Client and the Freelancer are each a "Party" and together the "Parties."

### Recitals

WHEREAS, the Client desires to retain the Freelancer to perform a complete visual identity redesign project described as "Brand Refresh 2025," which includes logo, color palette, typography, and a brand guidelines document; and WHEREAS, the Freelancer has the professional skills, experience, and expertise to perform the services described herein and is willing to provide such services on the terms and conditions set forth in this Agreement; NOW, THEREFORE, in consideration of the mutual promises and covenants contained herein, the Parties agree as follows.

---

## Clauses (23 total)

### Services

*Type: standard*

Freelancer will perform the services described in Exhibit A (the "Services") for the Project "Brand Refresh 2025." Services include creation and delivery of logo concepts, selected logo refinement, a brand guidelines document, and final asset package in accordance with the Deliverables, due dates, and specifications set forth in this Agreement.

### Deliverables, Schedule and Milestones

*Type: standard*

The Parties agree to the following Deliverables and Milestone Payments: (a) Logo concepts (3 options) — due January 30, 2025 — $2,000; (b) Selected logo refinement — due February 15, 2025 — $1,500; (c) Brand guidelines document — due March 1, 2025 — $2,500; (d) Asset package delivery — due March 15, 2025 — $2,500. The total contract amount is $8,500. Time is of the essence with respect to all due dates.

### Deposit and Payment Terms

*Type: standard*

Client shall pay Freelancer a non-refundable deposit of $2,550 (30% of total) upon execution of this Agreement. Remaining payments shall follow the milestone schedule: upon Client approval of Logo concepts — $2,000; upon approval of Selected logo refinement — $1,500; upon delivery and acceptance of Brand guidelines document — $2,500; upon delivery and acceptance of Asset package — $2,500, subject to the deposit already paid. All payments are due within fourteen (14) days of invoice. Invoices will be issued upon delivery of each milestone/deliverable.

### Payment Method and Late Payment

*Type: standard*

Payments shall be made in U.S. dollars by check, electronic transfer, or other mutually agreed method. Late payments will incur interest at 1.5% per month (or the maximum permitted by law, if less) on any unpaid balance from the due date until paid. Freelancer may suspend Services for overdue invoices after seven (7) days' written notice.

### Revision Rounds

*Type: standard*

Client is entitled to up to three (3) rounds of reasonably requested revisions per Deliverable as described herein. Additional revisions beyond three rounds or requests that materially change the scope will be billed at Freelancer's standard hourly rate and require a written change order.

### Acceptance

*Type: standard*

Deliverables shall be deemed accepted if Client does not provide written notice of non-conformity within seven (7) days of receipt. If Client provides timely notice, Freelancer shall correct non-conforming items within a mutually agreed timeframe and resubmit for acceptance.

### Change Orders

*Type: standard*

Any change to the scope, schedule, deliverables, or fees must be agreed in writing by both Parties via a written change order signed by authorized representatives of each Party. Change orders may affect fees and deadlines.

### Intellectual Property and Ownership

*Type: standard*

Upon full payment of all amounts due under this Agreement, Freelancer hereby assigns to Client all right, title, and interest in and to the final Deliverables created specifically for Client under this Agreement ("Final Deliverables"), excluding Freelancer's pre-existing materials, templates, tools, and stock assets for which Freelancer retains ownership. Freelancer grants Client a perpetual, worldwide, royalty-free license to use any Freelancer pre-existing materials incorporated in the Final Deliverables. Freelancer represents that, to Freelancer's knowledge, Final Deliverables will not infringe third-party intellectual property rights. Any third-party licensed assets included in Deliverables will be identified to Client and Client may be required to obtain separate licenses or pay additional fees. 

### Representations and Warranties

*Type: standard*

Each Party represents and warrants that it has the full power and authority to enter into this Agreement. Freelancer warrants that the Services will be performed in a professional manner consistent with industry standards. EXCEPT AS EXPRESSLY PROVIDED IN THIS SECTION, FREELANCER DISCLAIMS ALL OTHER WARRANTIES, EXPRESS OR IMPLIED, INCLUDING WARRANTIES OF MERCHANTABILITY AND FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE.

### Confidentiality

*Type: standard*

Each Party agrees to keep confidential the other Party's confidential information disclosed in connection with this Agreement and to use such information only for performing obligations under this Agreement. Confidential information does not include information that is or becomes publicly available through no breach of this Agreement or is independently developed by the receiving Party. The obligations in this Section survive termination for three (3) years.

### Independent Contractor

*Type: standard*

Freelancer is an independent contractor and not an employee, agent, or partner of Client. Freelancer is responsible for all taxes, withholdings, and other statutory obligations arising from the compensation paid under this Agreement. Client will not provide employee benefits to Freelancer.

### Expenses

*Type: negotiable*

Unless otherwise agreed in writing, Client will reimburse Freelancer for pre-approved out-of-pocket expenses reasonably incurred in connection with the Services upon presentation of receipts. If no pre-approval, Freelancer will not incur extraordinary expenses without Client's prior written consent.

### Taxes

*Type: standard*

Client will not withhold any taxes from payments made to Freelancer. Freelancer is solely responsible for all federal, state, and local taxes related to the payments received under this Agreement.

### Termination

*Type: standard*

Either Party may terminate this Agreement for convenience upon fourteen (14) days' written notice. Client may terminate for Freelancer's material breach if Freelancer fails to cure the breach within seven (7) days after written notice. Upon termination, Client will pay Freelancer for Services performed and expenses incurred through the effective date of termination, and Freelancer will deliver work-in-progress. The deposit is non-refundable but will be credited against amounts due for Services rendered. Sections dealing with payment, confidentiality, intellectual property, indemnification, and limitation of liability survive termination.

### Indemnification

*Type: standard*

Freelancer will indemnify and defend Client from third-party claims arising from Freelancer's gross negligence or willful misconduct in performing the Services. Client will indemnify and defend Freelancer from third-party claims arising from Client's use of the Final Deliverables, Client materials supplied to Freelancer, or Client's breach of this Agreement. Each Party's indemnity obligations are conditioned on prompt written notice of claims and reasonable cooperation in defense.

### Limitation of Liability

*Type: standard*

Except for liability arising from a Party's willful misconduct or gross negligence or its indemnification obligations, each Party's aggregate liability for claims arising out of or related to this Agreement will not exceed the total amount actually paid by Client to Freelancer under this Agreement. IN NO EVENT WILL EITHER PARTY BE LIABLE FOR CONSEQUENTIAL, INCIDENTAL, SPECIAL, OR PUNITIVE DAMAGES.

### Delays and Force Majeure

*Type: standard*

Neither Party will be liable for delays caused by events beyond its reasonable control ("Force Majeure"), including acts of God, labor disputes, governmental actions, or internet interruption. The affected Party will notify the other as soon as practicable and use reasonable efforts to resume performance. If a Force Majeure event continues for more than thirty (30) days, either Party may terminate upon written notice.

### Dispute Resolution and Governing Law

*Type: standard*

This Agreement is governed by the laws of the State of California without regard to its conflict of laws rules. The Parties will attempt in good faith to resolve disputes by negotiation. If unresolved within thirty (30) days, the Parties agree to non-binding mediation in California prior to initiating litigation. Either Party may seek equitable relief if necessary to protect intellectual property or confidential information. Venue for any litigation will be the state or federal courts located in California.

### Insurance

*Type: optional*

Freelancer is encouraged to maintain appropriate insurance (e.g., general liability, professional liability), but maintaining specific insurance is not a condition precedent to performance unless otherwise agreed in writing.

### Publicity

*Type: negotiable*

Freelancer may include limited descriptions and non-confidential images of the completed work in Freelancer's portfolio and promotional materials unless Client provides written objection. Client grants Freelancer a non-exclusive license to display Final Deliverables solely for Freelancer's marketing purposes unless Client and Freelancer agree otherwise in writing.

### Entire Agreement; Amendments

*Type: standard*

This Agreement, including any exhibits or attachments, constitutes the entire agreement between the Parties concerning the Services and supersedes all prior agreements and understandings. Any amendment must be in writing and signed by authorized representatives of both Parties.

### Notices

*Type: standard*

All notices must be in writing and delivered by email with confirmation, mailed by certified mail, or delivered by courier to the addresses provided by the Parties. Notices are effective upon receipt (or refusal of receipt).

### Severability

*Type: standard*

If any provision of this Agreement is held invalid or unenforceable, the remaining provisions will remain in full force and effect and the Parties will negotiate in good faith to replace the invalid provision with a valid provision that most closely approximates the intent and economic effect of the invalid provision.
